case_metadata.case_name: Huichun Feng v. Accord Physicians, PLLC
case_metadata.court: SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK, Appellate Division, Second Judicial Department

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On August 4, 2014, the then 70-year-old plaintiff presented at the offices of the defendants, Accord Physicians, PLLC, and Vitaly Raykhman, after being referred for an elevated level of prostate-specific antigen.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action to recover damages
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff presented to defendants on August 4, 2014, for elevated PSA.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On August 13, 2014, Raykhman performed a transrectal prostate biopsy on the plaintiff.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action to recover damages
evidence_index.summary: Transrectal prostate biopsy performed on August 13, 2014.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Following the procedure, the plaintiff used the bathroom and observed blood from his rectum in the toilet bowl.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action to recover damages
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff observed rectal bleeding after biopsy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Raykhman looked in the toilet bowl and relayed to the interpreter that this was a normal amount of blood from the procedure.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action to recover damages
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: An ambulance was called and the plaintiff was transported to the hospital, where he was given a blood transfusion and underwent surgery at the site of the biopsy to repair a laceration.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action to recover damages
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff required blood transfusion and surgery for biopsy site laceration.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The essential elements of medical malpractice are (1) a deviation or departure from accepted medical practice, and (2) evidence that such departure was a proximate cause of injury.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action to recover damages
evidence_index.summary: Court outlines elements of medical malpractice.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The defendants failed to establish their prima facie entitlement to summary judgment dismissing the complaint.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action to recover damages
evidence_index.summary: Defendants did not meet burden for summary judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The affidavit of the defendants' expert failed to address and rebut the specific allegations of malpractice set forth in the complaint and bill of particulars.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action to recover damages
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The Supreme Court should have denied that branch of their motion which was for summary judgment dismissing the cause of action alleging lack of informed consent.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action to recover damages
evidence_index.summary: Court reverses summary judgment on informed consent claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

verdict_detected.key_phrase: ORDERED that the order is reversed, on the law, with costs, and the defendants' motion for summary judgment dismissing the complaint is denied.

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented for evaluation of elevated PSA levels.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Transrectal Prostate Biopsy
requestables.description: Procedure note for biopsy performed on August 13, 2014

decision_points.prompt: How should the post-procedure bleeding be managed?

options.description: Monitor the patient and provide reassurance
Initiate further investigation and consider intervention
options.clinical_reasoning: Significant bleeding may indicate a complication requiring intervention

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant reassured patient that bleeding was normal

expected_action_court.description: Further investigation and intervention should have been considered

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Significant bleeding was not adequately addressed

decision_points.clinical_context: Informed consent for transrectal prostate biopsy

options.description: Informed consent was not adequately obtained
options.clinical_reasoning: Court found lack of informed consent

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant believed informed consent was adequate

expected_action_court.description: Informed consent was not adequately obtained
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Court reversed summary judgment on informed consent claim

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Informed consent was not adequately addressed

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents for elevated PSA evaluation

patient_outcome.description: Patient required blood transfusion and surgery for biopsy site laceration

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to address post-procedure bleeding

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to address post-procedure bleeding and lack of informed consent
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper management and informed consent could have prevented complications

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents for elevated PSA evaluation
